Home.heroTitle: Use Internet Safely, Identify Scams Easily.
Home.heroSubtitle: Verify any suspicious link or mobile number using our smart engine.

trending.title: Trending Scams
trending.subtitle: Latest fraudulent activities reported across the country.

items.title: Fake Investment App
items.desc: Apps promising 200% return in 24 hours. Reported 120 times.

items.title: BTRC Verification SMS
items.desc: SMS asking for sensitive data pretending to be BTRC.

items.title: WhatsApp Job Offer
items.desc: International numbers offering part-time remote work.

categories.title: Online Betting & Gambling
categories.desc: Promising 'guaranteed wins' or 'bonus' on fake betting sites.
categories.how: They use Facebook or YouTube ads to lure you into fake betting sites. Initially, they show small wins to build trust. Once you deposit a large amount, they block your ID or claim 'server down'.
categories.safety: Never invest money in unverified or unofficial betting apps. Remember, gambling sites are designed for you to lose.
categories.report: If you find a fake agent or your money is stuck, report the URL and agent number using our 'Report Scam' button.

categories.title: Fake Job & Recruitment
categories.desc: Using famous company logos to offer work-from-home jobs.
categories.how: They send messages for part-time jobs using famous logos. They claim you got the job without an interview but ask for a 'security deposit' or 'laptop fee'.
categories.safety: Official companies never ask for money before joining. Check if the email address is official (e.g., hr@company.com).
categories.report: If you receive a fake appointment letter, report it with a screenshot to warn others.

categories.title: Crypto & Investment
categories.desc: Promises of 'double your money' in 1 month via fake dashboards.
categories.how: Luring people with 'double money' or 'new crypto coins'. They show a fake dashboard where profits seem to increase, but you can never withdraw.
categories.safety: Check project whitepapers and global rankings. Never connect your wallet based on advice from Telegram 'experts'.
categories.report: Submit any suspicious wallet address or investment site to our portal.

categories.title: Phishing & Identity Theft
categories.desc: Fake websites mimicking banks to steal your PIN or Password.
categories.how: Hackers build exact replicas of bank or government sites. Once you enter your NID, PIN, or Password, they steal your data and empty your account.
categories.safety: Check domain names carefully before clicking (e.g., g00gle.com instead of google.com). Never give personal info on unknown links.
categories.report: Check suspicious links in our Verification Box and report if confirmed as a scam.

categories.title: Social Media & Extortion
categories.desc: Blackmailing users with edited videos or recorded calls.
categories.how: Strangers call on Imo or WhatsApp, record compromising videos (or edit them), and then demand money to prevent release.
categories.safety: Don't pick up video calls from strangers. Think twice before sharing personal photos. Enable Two-Factor Authentication.
categories.report: Upload blackmail messages or ID links to our secure Evidence Vault.

categories.title: Romance & Relationship
categories.desc: Posing as foreign beauties to demand 'customs clearance' fees.
categories.how: Scammers pose as foreigners, build trust over time, and claim they sent expensive gifts. Then, a fake 'customs officer' calls asking for 'duty' fees.
categories.safety: Never send money to someone you met online, especially for 'gift clearance' fees.
categories.report: Report such profiles or fake customs agent numbers on our website.

categories.title: Buying & Selling Fraud
categories.desc: Incredible low-price offers on electronics that never arrive.
categories.how: Offering phones or electronics at unbelievably low prices. They take advance payment via bKash/Nagad but never send the product or send fakes.
categories.safety: Use Cash on Delivery only. Check page reviews and age before ordering.
categories.report: Add fake page or website links to our database and file a report.

categories.title: Unexpected Money Scams
categories.desc: Claims of winning lotteries you never participated in.
categories.how: SMS or calls claiming you won a large lottery. They ask for advance 'VAT' or 'Tax' to release the prize.
categories.safety: You don't win a lottery you didn't buy. Just delete such messages.
categories.report: Report the calling number or SMS sender to our portal.

categories.title: AI Deepfake & Voice Scam
categories.desc: Using AI to mimic relative's voice for urgent money requests.
categories.how: Using AI to mimic a relative's voice. They call saying they are in an emergency or hospital and need money urgently.
categories.safety: Always verify by calling the person via another known method before sending money.
categories.report: Submit the calling number to our database.

categories.title: Immigration & Visa Fraud
categories.desc: Guaranteed visa advertisements by fake consultancy firms.
categories.how: Advertising '100% Guaranteed Visa' via fake firms. They take large advances and provide fake visas before disappearing.
categories.safety: Always contact official visa centers (e.g., VFS Global). Never give advance money to brokers.
categories.report: Report any fake agency or broker information to our portal.

Check.title: Smart Verification Engine

Check.privacy: Your privacy is protected. No personal data is stored during verification.

Recovery.title: Money Stolen? What to do now?
Recovery.subtitle: Follow these emergency steps to minimize damage.
Recovery.step1Desc: Call your bank or MFS (bKash/Nagad) helpline immediately to block your account.
Recovery.step2Desc: Go to your nearest police station and file a General Diary (GD) with evidence.
Recovery.step3Desc: Submit your report here with evidence to help police investigations.

Awareness.title: Awareness Hub
Awareness.subtitle: Correct use of knowledge is the biggest weapon against scams.
rules: Never share OTP or PIN with anyone.
Verify any offer on our portal before accepting.
Report immediately if anything seems suspicious.

Verification.reportDesc: This is a confirmed scam. Report to authorities to protect others.

Footer.description: The largest anti-scam portal in Bangladesh. Our goal is to keep citizens informed and safe through correct use of technology.
Footer.emergencyDesc: Contact your nearest police station immediately if you are a victim of a major cyber crime.
